channel.title: France 24 - International breaking news, top stories and headlines
channel.description: Breaking news and world news from France 24 on Business, Sports, Culture. Video news. News from the US, Europe, Asia Pacific, Africa, Middle East, America

item.title: EU's new rules on migration and asylum come into effect
item.description: Five years in the making, the EU's Pact on Migration and Asylum comes into effect this Friday. It aims to strengthen the bloc's external borders and incentivise cooperation between member countries on dealing with claims. Human rights groups, though, are warning the pact could add to the difficulties asylum-seekers face while trying to find safe haven in the EU.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: France accuses Israel’s BlackCore of meddling in elections around the world
item.description: Israeli firm BlackCore, suspected of interfering in France's local elections in March, is also suspected of meddling in elections in New York City and Scotland, and operating in Angola and Togo, France's disinformation detection service, Viginum, said on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: 'Filled with joy': Mexico celebrates victory in World Cup opener
item.description: There were wild celebrations in Mexico on Thursday after the country won 2-0 against 9-man South Africa in the opening game of the 2026 World Cup at Mexico City's Azteca Stadium, having previously failed to win on the opening day of the tournament on seven previous occasions.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: British artist David Hockney has died at 88, his agent says
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Celebrated British contemporary artist David Hockney dies at 88
item.description: British artist David Hockney has died at 88, his publicist said on Friday. Hockney, one of the most influential artists of the 20th and 21st centuries, worked in a range of mediums but is best known for his vivid use of colour in paintings depicting scenes from California, Normandy and the UK.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: As crises mount, how long can UK's Starmer survive?
item.description: British Defence Secretary John Healey resigned on Thursday, accusing Prime Minister Keir Starmer and the Treasury of failing to provide sufficient funding for the armed forces. The departure deepens pressure on Starmer ahead of a crucial by-election and comes amid anti-immigration riots in Belfast and a rise in support for the far-right. France 24s Philip Turle takes a look at the multiple crises piling up for Starmer and asks for how long he can cling to power?
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Trump touts imminent Iran deal as Tehran says no decision made
item.description: President Donald Trump said a deal with Iran could be imminent, touting a "great settlement" to end the war on Thursday, though Tehran said it had not reached a final decision on an agreement with Washington.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: ‘The future is peace’: An Israeli-Palestinian call for reconciliation
item.description: Aziz Abu Sarah is Palestinian. Maoz Inon is Israeli. Both their families have been bereaved by the endless conflict between their two communities. With their book "The Future is Peace" published in April, the two authors aim to rally international public opinion behind their project to put an end to the cycle of violence and find a path to reconciliation between Israelis and Palestinians. FRANCE 24 met them for an in-depth interview.
item.dc:creator: David GORMEZANO

item.title: Trump's World Cup: More matches, higher prices, fewer foreigners
item.description: Can blatant racism and shameless greed ruin the World Cup? Fans of the beautiful game and its history can take solace in the opening match at Mexico City's storied Azteca Stadium, the only venue to already host two World Cup finals.
item.dc:creator: François PICARD

item.title: 'What makes Gaudí and Sagrada Família so universal is precisely it being so local, so Catalan'
item.description: François Picard is pleased to welcome Antoni Vives, former Deputy Mayor of Barcelona in charge of urban planning. According to Vives, the Sagrada Família is not simply an architectural masterpiece. It is a living demonstration of how authenticity, rootedness, and transcendence can converge in a single human project. Its significance lies not only in its extraordinary design, but in its ability to connect the local with the universal, the contemporary with the eternal, and nature with all that's sacred.
item.dc:creator: François PICARD

item.title: Belfast unrest: 'Racists and groups allied to the far right are now unified across Europe'
item.description: François Picard is pleased to welcome Professor Peter Shirlow, Director of the Institute of Irish Studies at the University of Liverpool. Northern Ireland is once again confronting scenes of unrest, violence, and anti-migrant attacks. Shirlow frames the violence within a wider European and global context. He argues that today's far-right mobilisations are no longer isolated local phenomena but are increasingly interconnected through digital networks, transnational misinformation campaigns, and social media platforms that facilitate the rapid circulation of extremist narratives.
item.dc:creator: François PICARD

item.title: Funeral for former French first lady Bernadette Chirac to take place in Paris
item.description: The funeral of Bernadette Chirac, widow of former president Jacques Chirac, will take place on Friday at the Basilica of Sainte-Clotilde in Paris. Chirac died on June 5 at the age of 93.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: What's in the landmark EU migration reform hardening border protections?
item.description: A major EU immigration reform allowing member states to reduce protections for asylum-seekers comes into force on Friday. The changes have prompted right groups to warn that humanitarian concerns are taking a backseat to politics in Europe, where public opinion has hardened on migration.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: World Cup 2026: Mexico beat South Africa in opening match
item.description: The 2026 World Cup began in Mexico City with an opening ceremony filled with colour, dancing and a performance by global music star Shakira. Julian Quinones and Premier League player Raul Jimenez scored the first goals of the tournament.
item.dc:creator: L'Equipe TV

item.title: 'Acknowledging rights and needs of Israelis and Palestinians: Shared path towards a secure future'
item.description: François Picard is pleased to welcome Avi Meyerstein, co-founder and president of the Alliance for Middle East Peace (ALLMEP). At a moment when international attention is increasingly consumed by conflict across the Middle East, Meyer argues that the crises currently unfolding are not isolated events but manifestations of unresolved political conflicts that have been left to fester for decades. "While those things remain unresolved," he observes, any semblance of stability "is just an illusion."
item.dc:creator: François PICARD

item.title: South Korea sentences ex-president Yoon to 30 years over North Korea drone incident
item.description: South Korea on Friday sentenced ex-president Yoon Suk Yeol to 30 years in prison for sending military drones into North Korea in 2024 in a bid to "heighten inter-Korean military tensions and manufacture a national crisis" enabling him to declare martial law. Yeol was in February given a life imprisonment sentence – which he is appealing – for leading an insurrection with his martial law declaration.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: South Korea defeat Czech Republic to make strong World Cup start
item.description: South Korea won their first World Cup match on Thursday beating the Czech Republic 2-1, thanks to a decisive late goal from substitute Oh Hyeon-gyu.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Musk's SpaceX set to make biggest-ever initial public offering on Wall Street
item.description: Elon Musk's space transport company SpaceX is set to begin trading on the US market on Friday with an offering of more than 555 million shares priced at at $135 each. The move is expected to place SpaceX in the top 10 of Wall Street's biggest companies and make Musk the world's first trillionaire.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Trump to celebrate 80th birthday with cage fight on White House lawn
item.description: US President Donald Trump will celebrate his 80th birthday on Sunday with a characteristically unique event: a $60 million UFC match will see bare-knuckle fighters battle it out in a mesh-framed cage on the White House's famed South Lawn. Secretary of State Marco Rubio described the event as "a gift to the American people".
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Middle East live: US forces shoot down Iranian drones near Strait of Hormuz
item.description: US forces shot down two Iranian one-way attack drones as Tehran appeared to attempt to strike commercial ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz, hours after US President Donald Trump announced a "great settlement" to end the war. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: DR Congo's World Cup team cleared for US entry after Ebola quarantine
item.description: DR Congo coach Sebastien Desabre said Thursday he hopes his side can deliver a strong World Cup showing after arriving in the US following an imposed quarantine period linked to the Ebola outbreak. The team, making their first appearance at the tournament since 1974, have relocated preparations abroad but aim to bring some emotional relief to supporters back home.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: ‘Fuel on the fire’: Scientists confirm El Nino’s return, predict extreme weather effects
item.description: Meteorologists confirmed the arrival of El Nino in the Pacific Ocean on Thursday, warning that the climate pattern could grow into one of the strongest on record. Experts say it is likely to amplify global heat and fuel extreme weather including floods, droughts, wildfires and powerful storms in coming months.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Iran's football team trains in Mexico as World Cup campaign begins
item.description: Iran's national football team held its first open training session in Mexico on Thursday, offering a glimpse of a squad preparing for the World Cup under the shadow of conflict with the US as President Donald Trump claimed a breakthrough in efforts to end the war.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: World Cup 2026: Mexican fans celebrate first win
item.description: #Mexico won 2-0 against 9-man #SouthAfrica in the opening game of the 2026 #WorldCup at Mexico City's #Azteca Stadium.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24
